title: Three Lectures On Topological Phases Of Matter
canonical_scope: Lecture One only in the present repository version.

section_map.title: Relativistic Dispersion In One Space Dimension
section_map.summary: Linearization near a Fermi point yields chiral low-energy modes in 1D.

section_map.title: Three Dimensions
section_map.summary: Generic isolated two-band crossings in 3D are locally Weyl points.

section_map.title: The Nielsen-Ninomiya Theorem
section_map.summary: The total topological charge of isolated Weyl nodes on a lattice vanishes.

section_map.title: The Berry Connection
section_map.summary: Occupied states define a line bundle whose curvature measures node charge.

section_map.title: Some Examples
section_map.summary: Toy Hamiltonians create, move, split, and annihilate Weyl nodes.

section_map.title: Band Crossing At The Fermi Energy
section_map.summary: A physically relevant semimetal requires the crossing to sit at the Fermi level.

section_map.title: Including Spin
section_map.summary: Spin doubles bands and changes symmetry bookkeeping.

section_map.title: A System With Many Bands
section_map.summary: Berry connections become non-abelian before projection to filled states.

section_map.title: Two Dimensions
section_map.summary: Generic 2D Dirac crossings require special structure or symmetry.

section_map.title: Weyl Fermions And Fermi Arcs
section_map.summary: Surface spectra terminate at the projections of bulk Weyl nodes.

section_map.title: Gapless Boundary Modes From Dirac Fermions
section_map.summary: Mass-domain-wall intuition explains protected boundary-localized gapless modes.

section_map.title: Discrete Lattice Symmetries and Massless Dirac Fermions
section_map.summary: Massless Dirac points survive in lattices only when symmetry data enforce them.

section_map.title: Simple Examples Of Band Hamiltonians
section_map.summary: Periodic lattice Hamiltonians exhibit fermion doubling in explicit models.
